description: Send notes to webhook endpoints, for seamless integration with i.a. n8n, Make.com, and Zapier.

name: Post Webhook

releases.author: Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog v1.2.5

- Fixed Property Type Handling (Boolean)
- Added Raw Content Option

releases.author: Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog for v1.2.4

### New Features

*   **Send Rendered HTML:** You can now configure a webhook to send the rendered HTML of a note from Reading View, for including dynamic content from plugins like Dataview and Bases in your webhook payloads.
*   **Advanced Response Handling with Attachments:** If your webhook returns a JSON object with a `content` key and an `attachments` array (containing files with `name` and base64 `data`), the plugin will automatically save the attachments to your vault and insert the text content into your note.
*   **Configurable Request Timeout:** Set a custom timeout (in seconds) for each webhook to prevent requests from hanging indefinitely.
*   **Convert Internal Links to Obsidian URIs:** A new option allows you to automatically convert internal `[[wiki-links]]` into full `obsidian://` URIs, making them accessible from outside your vault.
*   **Sending Status Indicator:** A visual indicator is now shown in the status bar while a webhook is being sent, providing better feedback on the request's progress.

### Improvements

*   **Better Error Messages:** Error handling has been improved to provide more descriptive messages for common issues, such as 404 (Not Found) or 405 (Method Not Allowed) errors.
*   **Attachment Processing Notification:** You will now receive a notice if the plugin fails to process an attachment, making it easier to debug issues.
*   **Updated Dependencies:** The project's build tools have been updated for better performance and security.

releases.author: Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog v1.2.3

- Webhook payloads now include additional file metadata:
    - filepath: Full file path within the vault
    - createdAt: File creation timestamp
    - modifiedAt: File last modified timestamp

releases.author: Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog v1.2.2

- Added ability to copy command IDs for use with Templater templates and other plugins
- Added support for custom headers in webhook requests for authentication and specialized endpoints

releases.author: Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog v1.2.1

- Enhanced UI with improved webhook management interface
- New response handling options: append, overwrite, or create new note
- Support for inline fields (field:: value) in webhook parameters
- Various minor improvements and optimizations

releases.author: Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog v1.2.0

- Complete rewrite in TypeScript for improved reliability and maintainability
- Added "Exclude Attachments" option in settings to skip sending attachments for specific Webhooks
- Introduced Context Notes feature for adding reusable content blocks to Webhook requests

releases.author: Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog v1.1.2

- Added option to exclude attachments from payload in YAML.

releases.author: Masterb1234
Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog v1.1.0

- Added support for configuring and managing multiple Webhook endpoints
- Introduced text selection support, allowing users to send selected portions of notes to Webhooks; responses are inserted directly after selections
- Fixed various bugs and improved overall stability

releases.author: Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog v1.0.3
- Added new "Attach Response" toggle in settings that allows webhook responses to be automatically appended to the note with a separator line
- Improved HTTP request handling by using Obsidian's native requestUrl API instead of fetch

releases.author: Masterb1234
releases.description:
## Changelog v1.0.2
- Switched to Obsidian's native parseYaml function for YAML parsing
- Replaced custom Base64 conversion with Obsidian's arrayBufferToBase64
- Updated HTTP requests to use Obsidian's requestUrl instead of fetch
- Implemented native getFrontMatterInfo for frontmatter parsing
- Improved attachment handling using Obsidian's file cache and link resolution
- Refactored parseYamlFrontmatter to use Obsidian's built-in functions
- Minor other changes
